name: Permutive Audience Platform API
what_it_does: Privacy-first audience platform with REST API — enabling publishers and advertisers to build, activate, and monetize first-party audiences without third-party cookies, using real-time edge-based audience segmentation that processes data in-browser, with data clean room for secure publisher-advertiser collaboration.
use_cases: Building real-time audience segments from publisher first-party data without cookies
Activating publisher audiences to DSPs and advertising platforms via Permutive API
Setting up advertiser-publisher data collaboration via Permutive clean room
Querying audience segment sizes and behavioral data for publisher yield reporting
Integrating Permutive with DMP/CDP for unified first-party data strategy
not_for: Cookie-based targeting (Permutive is specifically built for cookieless environments)
Small publishers (<1M monthly users) — audience platform ROI requires meaningful scale
Non-publisher companies — Permutive's core value is for media and content publishers
best_when: A premium content publisher needs to build and monetize first-party audiences in a cookieless environment — replacing third-party cookie revenue with privacy-safe, consent-based audience activation for programmatic advertising.
avoid_when: You're an advertiser (not a publisher), rely primarily on third-party data and cookies, or have low traffic making first-party audience scale insufficient for programmatic value.

auth.notes: API key from Permutive dashboard. Bearer token auth. Project-scoped access. Webhook for segment update events. JavaScript SDK for in-browser audience collection (edge processing). REST API for segment management and reporting. Real-time data collection via Permutive JS tag on publisher pages. No OAuth2.

pricing.paid_tiers: CPM-based or platform fee; contact sales; typically $50K-500K+/year
pricing.notes: London company. Founded 2014. Raised $75M Series C (2021). Backed by EQT Ventures, Softbank, Octopus Ventures. Privacy-first positioning is core differentiator — all processing in-browser (edge), no third-party cookies. 100+ publisher customers including CNN, Condé Nast, Bloomberg. Clean room enables advertiser-publisher collaboration without data sharing. GDPR-first by design (UK/EU company). TCF2 consent management built in.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per project; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for segment management, audience reporting, and clean room configuration. API key auth. Good developer documentation. JavaScript SDK is primary data collection mechanism — REST API manages configuration and reporting. Edge processing model (in-browser) limits server-side API visibility into individual user data. Webhook for segment updates. TCF2 consent required for EU audience activation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Segment reads fully idempotent. Report reads idempotent by period. Clean room query reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: In-browser (edge) processing — JavaScript SDK required on publisher site for data collection
Privacy model: individual user data never leaves browser — only aggregated segments via API
TCF2 consent management mandatory for EU activation — configure before going live
Clean room collaboration requires advertiser also on Permutive — bilateral setup
ePrivacy Directive cookie consent required in EU/UK for JavaScript tag placement

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, CCPA, TCF2, ePrivacy compliant. EU and US data residency. API key auth. Privacy-by-design architecture — individual user data processed in-browser, never sent to server. UK company with GDPR-first design. TCF2 consent chain required for all EU programmatic activation. ePrivacy Directive compliance for cookie/tag placement.
